question_text: By next Friday, my brother ________ in this company for five years.
explanation: Bu yerda 'qancha vaqt?' (five years) davom etayotgan jarayon ta'kidlangan, shuning uchun Future Perfect Continuous ishlatiladi.

question_text: By the end of the day, she _____ (write) all the emails.
explanation: Ish-harakat natijasi (barcha xatlarni yozib bo'lishi) muhim bo'lgani uchun Future Perfect ishlatiladi.

question_text: By the time we reach the station, the train ________.
explanation: Poyezd jo'nab ketishi tugallangan ish hisoblanadi, shuning uchun 'will have' + V3 ishlatiladi.

question_text: By next month, I _____ (work) in this company for two years.
explanation: Gapda 'for two years' davomiyligi mavjud. Ish-harakatning kelajakda qancha vaqtdan beri davom etayotganini ko'rsatish uchun Future Perfect Continuous ishlatiladi.

question_text: By next month, I ________ this project for three weeks.
explanation: Ishning qancha vaqt (for three weeks) davom etganiga e'tibor qaratilgani uchun Future Perfect Continuous to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: By midnight, we _____ (clean) the whole house.
explanation: Gapda uy tozalab bo'lingani (natija) haqida so'z ketmoqda, shuning uchun Future Perfect ishlatiladi.

question_text: By midnight, the children ________ for six hours.
explanation: Bolalar qancha vaqt uxlab yotganini (for six hours) ko'rsatganimiz uchun Future Perfect Continuous mos keladi.

question_text: By 9 AM tomorrow, they _____ (travel) for 24 hours.
explanation: Harakatning qancha vaqt davom etishi ('for 24 hours') ko'rsatilgani uchun jarayonni ifodalovchi Future Perfect Continuous to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: By the end of this year, she ________ all her exams.
explanation: Imtihonlarni topshirish tugallangan ish hisoblanadi, shuning uchun Future Perfect (will have V3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: By Friday, I _____ (read) this book.
explanation: Kitobni o'qib tugatish natija hisoblanadi, shuning uchun Future Perfect shakli to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: By 6 o'clock, we ________ for the concert for two hours.
explanation: Jarayonning qancha vaqt davom etayotganini (for two hours) ta'kidlaganimiz uchun Future Perfect Continuous tanlanadi.

question_text: By the time we arrive, the children _____ (play) for three hours.
explanation: 'For three hours' davomiylikni bildiradi, demak biz jarayonga e'tibor qaratishimiz kerak. Bu holatda Future Perfect Continuous ishlatiladi.

question_text: Don't worry, by the time you wake up, I ________ the breakfast.
explanation: Nonushtani tayyorlab qo'yish yakunlangan ish bo'lgani uchun 'will have' + V3 ishlatiladi.

question_text: By next year, my sister _____ (study) piano for five years.
explanation: Besh yillik davomiylik ('for five years') haqida so'z borayotgani uchun Future Perfect Continuous to'g'ri tanlovdir.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence focusing on the duration: 'By May, I ________ in this house for ten years.'
explanation: O'n yil (for ten years) davomiylikni anglatadi, shuning uchun jarayonga urg'u beruvchi Future Perfect Continuous ishlatiladi.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence to emphasize the 'result':
options: I will have been painting the wall by noon.
I will have painted the wall by noon.
I will be painting the wall by noon.
I am painting the wall by noon.
correct_answer: I will have painted the wall by noon.
explanation: Kartochkaga ko'ra, 'natija' uchun Future Perfect (will have + V3) ishlatiladi, 'jarayon' uchun esa Continuous.

question_text: Identify the sentence where the result is the main focus: 'By tomorrow, I ________ the report.'
explanation: Hisobotni yozib tugatish natija hisoblanadi, shuning uchun Future Perfect (will have + V3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Identify the correct completion: 'By 5 PM, he _____ (cook) dinner for two hours.'
explanation: Gapda 'for two hours' iborasi borligi uni davomiylikni bildiruvchi Future Perfect Continuous zamoniga mos kelishini ko'rsatadi.

question_text: When I was young, I ____ play the piano very well.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, o'tmishdagi qobiliyatlarni ifodalash uchun 'could' modal fe'lidan foydalanamiz.

question_text: I ________ speak English quite well right now.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, hozirgi zamondagi qobiliyat uchun 'can' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Look at the dark clouds! It ____ rain soon.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'may' biroz aniqroq ehtimol uchun ishlatiladi, bulutlar esa yomg'ir yog'ishiga aniqroq ishora qiladi.

question_text: When my brother was a little boy, he ________ jump very high.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, o'tmishdagi doimiy qobiliyat uchun 'could' ishlatiladi.

question_text: I ____ play football every weekend now.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, hozirgi zamondagi qobiliyatni ifodalash uchun 'can'dan foydalanamiz.

question_text: Look at the dark clouds! It ________ rain, but I am not sure.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'might' uzoqroq, kamroq ehtimolni ifodalaydi.

question_text: I am not sure if I can come to the party. I ____ be late.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, juda noaniq ehtimollar uchun 'might' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Next year, I ________ graduate from the university.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, 'can' faqat hozirgi zamonda ishlatiladi, kelasi zamon uchun esa 'be able to' kerak.

question_text: After the training, you ____ speak English fluently next month.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, kelasi zamon haqida gap ketganda 'will be able to' shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: I haven't decided yet, but I ________ visit my grandmother this weekend.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'may' biroz aniqroq (taxminan 50%) ehtimolni bildiradi.

question_text: My grandfather ____ run very fast when he was a teenager.
explanation: Bu o'tmishdagi qobiliyat bo'lgani uchun 'could' modal fe'li to'g'ri tanlovdir.

question_text: Many years ago, people ________ travel by ship across the ocean.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, o'tmishdagi qobiliyat uchun 'can'ning o'tgan zamon shakli 'could' ishlatiladi.

question_text: He ____ visit us tonight, but he hasn't decided yet.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, imkoniyat juda noaniq yoki past bo'lganda 'might' ishlatiladi.

question_text: She ________ solve this math problem right now because she is very smart.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, hozirgi zamonda qobiliyat uchun 'can' yoki 'be able to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: If you study hard, you ____ pass the exam tomorrow.
explanation: Kelasi zamondagi qobiliyatni ifodalash uchun kartochka 1 ga asosan 'will be able to' qo'llaniladi.

question_text: In the future, technology ________ help us communicate faster.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, 'be able to' zamon tanlamaydi va kelasi zamonda ham qo'llanilishi mumkin.

question_text: In the past, people ____ send emails, but now it is very easy.
explanation: Kartochka 2 'could' o'tmishdagi qobiliyat ekanligini aytadi; inkor shakli esa o'tmishdagi qobiliyatsizlikni bildiradi.

question_text: It is just a theory, so it ________ be true or false.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, biror narsa sodir bo'lishi ehtimolini bildirish uchun 'might'dan foydalanamiz.

question_text: Tomorrow, I ____ finish the report because I have a lot of free time.
explanation: Kelasi zamonda aniq qobiliyat nazarda tutilgani uchun Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra 'will be able to' eng mos keladi.

question_text: Before I broke my leg, I ________ run for an hour without stopping.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, o'tmishdagi doimiy ravishda bajara olgan ish haqida gapirganda 'could' ishlatiladi.

question_text: The sign says 'No entry'. You ___ enter this room.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, qat'iyan man etilgan yoki xavfli ishlarda 'mustn't' ishlatiladi. 'No entry' belgisi kirish taqiqlanganini anglatadi.

question_text: The sign says 'No Parking'. You ________ park your car here.
explanation: Kartochka 3-ga ko'ra, agar biror ish qat'iyan taqiqlangan bo'lsa yoki qoida bo'lsa, 'mustn't' ishlatamiz.

question_text: We ___ wear seatbelts in the car because it is the law.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, tashqi qoida yoki sharoit taqozosi bilan bajariladigan ishlar uchun 'have to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: I have an empty fridge, so I ________ go to the grocery store today.
explanation: Kartochka 2-ga ko'ra, shaxsiy ehtiyojingiz bilan bog'liq ishlarni qilish uchun 'need to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: It is a sunny day. You ___ take your coat with you.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, agar biror narsani qilish shart bo'lmasa, 'needn't' ishlatiladi. Quyoshli kunda palto olish shart emas.

question_text: We ________ wear a tie at work because it is a strict company policy.
explanation: Kartochka 1-ga ko'ra, tashqi qoida yoki sharoit taqozosi bilan qilinadigan majburiyatlar uchun 'have to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: You ___ park your car here; it is strictly forbidden.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, biror ish taqiqlangan bo'lsa, biz 'mustn't' ishlatamiz.

question_text: The weather is sunny, so you ________ take your coat with you.
explanation: Kartochka 4-ga ko'ra, biror ishni qilish shart bo'lmasa, ya'ni majburiyat bo'lmasa, 'needn't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: The library is very quiet. You ___ shout here.
explanation: Kutubxonada baqirish taqiqlangan xatti-harakat hisoblanadi, shuning uchun qat'iy taqiqni bildiruvchi 'mustn't' mos keladi.

question_text: You ________ touch the exhibits in the museum. It is strictly forbidden.
explanation: Muzeydagi qoidalar tashqi taqiq hisoblanadi, shuning uchun 'mustn't' to'g'ri javob hisoblanadi.

question_text: The meeting is optional. You ___ attend it if you are busy.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga binoan, biror narsani qilish shart bo'lmasa yoki tanlov imkoni bo'lsa, 'needn't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: My office has a strict dress code. I ___ wear a tie every day.
explanation: Bu tashqi qoida bo'lgani uchun majburiyatni bildiruvchi 'have to' ishlatilishi kerak (Kartochka 1).

question_text: You ________ pay for the entrance ticket; it is free for everyone today.
explanation: Kirish bepul bo'lgani uchun pul to'lash shart emas, bu holatda 'needn't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: You ___ feed the animals at the zoo; there is a sign that forbids it.
explanation: Hayvonot bog'ida hayvonlarni boqish taqiqlangan ish hisoblanadi, shuning uchun 'mustn't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: I feel very tired. I ________ get some sleep immediately.
explanation: Charchoq bu shaxsiy tana ehtiyojidir, shuning uchun bu yerda 'need to' eng mos keladi.

question_text: We have plenty of food at home, so you ___ go to the shop today.
explanation: Uyda ovqat yetarli bo'lgani uchun do'konga borish shart emas. Zaruriyat yo'qligini 'needn't' ifodalaydi.

question_text: Look at the sign: 'Silence please'. You ________ talk loudly in the library.
explanation: Kutubxonada baland ovozda gapirish qoida bo'yicha taqiqlangan, shuning uchun 'mustn't' ishlatamiz.

question_text: The rules are very clear. Every student ___ arrive at school by 8:00 AM.
explanation: Maktab qoidalari tashqi majburiyatdir, shuning uchun bu holatda 'have to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: The train is already waiting, but we have plenty of time. We ________ run.
explanation: Vaqt ko'p bo'lgani uchun yugurish majburiyat emas, shuning uchun 'needn't'dan foydalanamiz.

question_text: Look at those dark clouds! It _______ rain soon.
explanation: Since there is a possibility (around 50%) based on the clouds, 'might' is the correct choice for a guess.

question_text: Look at the sky! It is full of dark clouds. It ___ rain soon.
explanation: Bulutlar borligi rain (yomg'ir) yog'ishi mumkinligini bildiradi, lekin bu 100% emas, shuning uchun 'might' (50/50 ehtimol) ishlatiladi.

question_text: He has been studying medicine for six years, so he _______ know a lot about the human body.
explanation: We are 90-100% sure because of the logical evidence that he has studied for a long time.

question_text: She has won the gold medal in the Olympics. She ___ be very happy.
explanation: Oltin medal yutgan insonning juda baxtli bo'lishi mantiqiy xulosa (100% aniq fakt), shuning uchun 'must' ishlatamiz.

question_text: That man is a stranger; he _______ be your brother.
explanation: It is logically impossible for a stranger to be your brother, so we use 'can't'.

question_text: A: Is that John? B: No, it ___ be John. He is in London right now.
explanation: Agar u Londonda bo'lsa, u hozir shu yerda bo'lishi imkonsiz. Imkonsiz narsalar uchun 'can't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: I am not sure where my phone is. It _______ be in my bag or in the kitchen.
explanation: Since the location is uncertain (50% chance), we use 'could' to express a possibility.

question_text: Where is my phone? It ___ be in the bag, or it ___ be on the table.
explanation: Telefonning qayerdaligi aniq emas, ikkita variant ham bo'lishi mumkinligi uchun noaniq taxmin 'could' ishlatiladi.

question_text: She has been practicing the piano every day for years. She _______ be a very good player.
explanation: Consistent practice provides strong logical evidence that she is skilled, making 'must' the right choice.

question_text: He studied all night for the exam. He ___ be very tired today.
explanation: Butun tun o'qigan odamning charchashi tabiiy va mantiqiy xulosa, shuning uchun 'must' tanlanadi.

question_text: This restaurant is famous and usually crowded. It _______ be empty on a Friday night.
explanation: Because it is famous and usually busy, it is logically impossible for it to be empty, so we use 'can't'.

question_text: I don't know why she is crying. She ___ be sad, or she ___ be just emotional.
explanation: Sabablar noaniq, har ikkalasi ham bo'lishi mumkin, shuning uchun 'might' (50/50 ehtimol) to'g'ri.

question_text: A: Where is your boss? B: I'm not certain. He _______ be in a meeting or in his office.
explanation: Since the person is unsure about the boss's location, 'might' is used to express an uncertain possibility.

question_text: They have been married for 50 years. They ___ know each other very well.
explanation: 50 yillik turmush tajribasi bir-birini yaxshi bilishini 100% aniq xulosa qiladi, shuning uchun 'must' ishlatiladi.

question_text: You have just had a big dinner. You _______ be hungry again already!
explanation: It is impossible to be hungry immediately after a big meal, so we use 'can't' for a negative logical conclusion.

question_text: You just had a huge lunch. You ___ be hungry again already!
explanation: Yangi ovqatlangan odamning darhol yana och qolishi imkonsiz, shuning uchun 'can't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: The lights are off and the front door is locked. They _______ be at home right now.
explanation: The locked door and lights being off serve as logical evidence that it is impossible for them to be at home.

question_text: Look at the person wearing a winter coat in this hot weather! He ___ be feeling very hot.
explanation: Issiq ob-havoda qishki palto kiyish issiqlikni keltirib chiqarishi mantiqiy fakt, shuning uchun 'must' ishlatamiz.

question_text: I can't find my umbrella. It _______ be at the office or left in the taxi.
explanation: The speaker is uncertain about the location of the umbrella, so 'might' is used to show a 50% possibility.

question_text: I can't find my keys. They ___ be in the kitchen or in the car.
explanation: Kalitlar qayerdaligi haqida aniq ma'lumot yo'q, shuning uchun 'might' (noaniq taxmin) eng mosidir.

question_text: Last night, I ________ finish my project because my boss requested it.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, o'tmishda bajarilishi shart bo'lgan ishlar uchun 'had to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Choose the correct option: I ______ finish my homework last night because the teacher asked for it.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga binoan, o'tgan zamondagi majburiyatni ifodalash uchun 'must' emas, balki 'had to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: When my grandfather was twenty, he ________ run a marathon.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'could' o'tmishdagi umumiy qobiliyatni bildiradi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: When my father was young, he ______ play the guitar very well.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, uzoq vaqt davomida egallangan umumiy qobiliyatlarni ifodalash uchun 'could' ishlatiladi.

question_text: The lock was very old, but finally I ________ open it.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, aniq bir qiyin vaziyatda muvaffaqiyatli bajarilgan ish uchun 'was able to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Yesterday, I ______ work late to finish the report.
explanation: O'tgan zamondagi majburiyat haqida gap ketganda, barcha shaxslar uchun 'had to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: I ________ walk to school every morning last year because the bus didn't come.
explanation: O'tmishdagi majburiyatni ifodalash uchun 'had to' fe'li ishlatiladi.

question_text: My grandmother ______ speak three languages when she lived in her home country.
explanation: Tillarni bilish bu umumiy qobiliyat hisoblanadi, shuning uchun 'could' to'g'ri javobdir.

question_text: She ________ play the piano beautifully when she was a little girl.
explanation: Bu gap umumiy qobiliyat haqida ketmoqda, shuning uchun 'could' mos keladi.

question_text: The fire was dangerous, but the firemen ______ save everyone in the building.
explanation: Bu aniq vaziyatdagi (bir martalik) qiyin ish va muvaffaqiyat bo'lgani uchun 'were able to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: It was dark, but he ________ find the way out of the forest.
explanation: Bu aniq bir qiyin vaziyatdagi muvaffaqiyat bo'lgani uchun 'was able to' to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: I lost my keys, but I ______ find them after searching for an hour.
explanation: Bu aniq vaziyatda muvaffaqiyatga erishilgan ish bo'lgani uchun 'was able to' ishlatilishi to'g'riroq.

question_text: We ________ pay the fine yesterday because we parked in the wrong place.
explanation: Jarimani to'lash majburiyat bo'lgani sababli 'had to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Last week, we ______ clean the whole house before our guests arrived.
explanation: Uyni tozalash bu yerda majburiyatni bildiradi, shuning uchun 'had to' ishlatiladi.

question_text: In his youth, he ________ swim across the river easily.
explanation: Bu umumiy qobiliyatni ifodalaydi, shuning uchun 'could' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Even though the exam was extremely difficult, she ______ pass it with a high score.
explanation: Qiyin vaziyatdagi muvaffaqiyat ta'kidlangani uchun 'was able to' mos keladi.

question_text: The exam was very difficult, but at the end, I ________ finish it on time.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, aniq bir qiyin vaziyatda ishni bajara olganimiz uchun 'was able to' tanlanadi.

question_text: Which sentence is grammatically correct based on the rules provided?
options: I could study yesterday because I had time.
I was able to lift the heavy box yesterday.
I had to could go there.
correct_answer: I was able to lift the heavy box yesterday.
explanation: Boshqa variantlarda 'must'ning o'tgan zamoni noto'g'ri yozilgan yoki modal fe'llar noto'g'ri biriktirilgan. 'Was able to' aniq vaziyatdagi qobiliyat uchun to'g'ri ishlatilgan.

question_text: I ________ leave the party early last night because I had an early flight.
explanation: Bu o'tmishdagi zarurat/majburiyat bo'lgani uchun 'had to' to'g'ri javob hisoblanadi.

question_text: Select the sentence where 'was able to' is used correctly to show a successful outcome of a difficult situation.
options: I was able to run when I was five.
The window was stuck, but I was able to open it.
I was able to eat breakfast every morning.
I was able to speak English well last year.
correct_answer: The window was stuck, but I was able to open it.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'was able to' aniq, qiyin vaziyatda muvaffaqiyatga erishilganda ishlatiladi.

question_text: I failed the test because I didn't study. I _______ harder.
explanation: O'tmishdagi xato yoki bajarilmay qolgan ish uchun afsuslanish ifodalanganda 'should have + V3' qolipidan foydalaniladi.

question_text: I didn't lock the door. Now I regret it. I ____ the door.
explanation: Afsuslanish va o'tmishda qilinmagan ishni ifodalash uchun 'should have + V3' qolipidan foydalanamiz.

question_text: Look at his happy face! He _______ the lottery.
explanation: Vaziyatga nisbatan 90-100% amin bo'lib, mantiqiy xulosa chiqarayotganda 'must have + V3' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Look at his wet clothes! He ____ in the rain.
explanation: Kiyimlarining ho'lligiga qarab, uning yomg'irda yurganiga 90-100% aminmiz, shuning uchun 'must have + V3' ishlatiladi.

question_text: She left an hour ago, so she _______ arrived at the office yet.
explanation: Biror ish o'tgan zamonda yuz berishi imkonsiz deb hisoblanganda 'can't have + V3' qolipidan foydalaniladi.

question_text: She arrived at the airport, but she forgot her passport. She ____ it.
explanation: Pasportni olib kelmagani xato bo'lgani uchun va u buni qilishi kerak edi deb hisoblaganimiz uchun 'should have + V3' to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: I didn't invite him to the party, and now I feel bad. I _______ him.
explanation: O'tmishda qilinishi kerak bo'lgan, lekin qilinmagan ish uchun afsuslanish 'should have + V3' orqali ifodalanadi.

question_text: The lights are off and the house is silent. They ____ out.
explanation: Chiroqlar o'chig'i va uyda hech kim yo'qligi ularning ketganiga aniq ishonchimizni bildiradi, shuning uchun 'must have + V3' ishlatamiz.

question_text: The lights are off and nobody is answering the phone. They _______ out.
explanation: Chiroqlar o'chiqligi va javob yo'qligi asosida vaziyatga 90-100% ishonch bilan xulosa chiqarish uchun 'must have + V3' mos keladi.

question_text: John is a vegetarian, so he ____ the chicken last night.
explanation: Jon vegetarian bo'lgani uchun tovuq go'shtini yegan bo'lishi mutlaqo imkonsiz, shuning uchun 'can't have + V3' ishlatiladi.

question_text: He said he was at home all day, so he _______ at the cinema yesterday.
explanation: Uyda bo'lgan odamning kinoteatrda bo'lishi imkonsiz, shuning uchun 'can't have + V3' ishlatiladi.

question_text: You look very tired today. You ____ more sleep last night.
explanation: Tarcharchagan holatiga qarab, u ko'proq uxlashi kerak edi deb afsuslanish yoki maslahat berish ma'nosida 'should have + V3' ishlatamiz.

question_text: It was a mistake to sell my old car. I _______ it.
explanation: O'tmishdagi qaror uchun afsuslanishni bildirishda 'should have + V3' qolipidan foydalanamiz.

question_text: They were at home all evening. They ____ to the cinema.
explanation: Ular butun oqshom uyda bo'lishgan, demak, kinoga borgan bo'lishlari imkonsiz. Qat'iy ishonchsizlik uchun 'can't have + V3' ishlatamiz.

question_text: She knows everything about the project; she _______ the report very carefully.
explanation: Hamma narsani bilishi uning hisobotni diqqat bilan o'qiganiga 90-100% ishonchni bildiradi, shuning uchun 'must have + V3' tanlanadi.

question_text: He passed all his exams with the highest scores. He ____ very hard.
explanation: Yuqori natija uning qattiq tayyorlanganiga 90-100% ishonch hosil qiladi, shu sababli 'must have + V3' to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: They promised to be here by 5:00. It is 8:00 now. They _______ lost their way.
explanation: Soat 8:00 bo'lgani va ular hali kelmagani uchun, adashib qolganliklari haqidagi mantiqiy xulosa 'must have + V3' bilan ifodalanadi.

question_text: I failed the test because I didn't study enough. I ____ harder.
explanation: O'tmishdagi xatodan afsuslanish ('attang' ma'nosi) bildirilayotgani uchun 'should have + V3' qolipi ishlatiladi.

question_text: You _______ me earlier that you were coming; I would have prepared dinner.
explanation: Kech xabar berilgani uchun afsuslanish ifodalangan, ya'ni 'oldin aytishingiz kerak edi' ma'nosi uchun 'should have + V3' kerak.

question_text: Tom has never been to France. He ____ the French museum last week.
explanation: Tom hech qachon Fransiyada bo'lmagan, shuning uchun o'tgan hafta Fransiyadagi muzeyga borgan bo'lishi imkonsiz. 'Can't have + V3' qat'iy ishonchsizlikni bildiradi.

question_text: Choose the correct question form for 'They are ready'.
explanation: To be fe'li (are) so'roq gap yasash uchun egadan (they) oldinga o'tkaziladi.

question_text: Select the correct option: '___ you like chocolate?'
explanation: 'Like' harakat fe'li bo'lgani uchun savol yasashda 'do' yordamchi fe'lidan foydalaniladi.

question_text: Choose the correct negative sentence: 'I ___ drink coffee'.
explanation: I, you, we, they olmoshlari bilan harakat fe'lini inkor qilishda 'don't' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Choose the correct question: '___ he read books?'
explanation: Uchinchi shaxs birligi 'he' uchun harakat fe'li so'rog'ida 'does' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Find the correct negative sentence for: 'He eats apples'.
explanation: 'Does' ishlatilganda asosiy fe'ldagi -s qo'shimchasi tushib qoladi, shuning uchun 'eat' shakli to'g'ri.

question_text: Which sentence is correct?
explanation: 'Hungry' sifat bo'lgani uchun 'to be' fe'lining 'is' shakli bilan so'roq yasaladi.

question_text: Transform into a negative sentence: 'They play tennis'.
explanation: Harakat fe'li (play) bilan inkor yasashda 'do not' (don't) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Identify the grammatically correct question.
explanation: 'She' uchun 'does' ishlatiladi va 'does' ishlatilganda fe'lda -s qo'shimchasi bo'lmaydi.

question_text: Choose the correct negative version of 'The cat runs'.
explanation: 'The cat' (it) uchun 'doesn't' ishlatiladi va asosiy fe'ldagi -s qo'shimchasi olib tashlanadi.

question_text: If you mix red and blue, you _______ purple.
explanation: Bu tabiiy qonuniyat (Zero Conditional) bo'lgani uchun har ikki qismda ham Present Simple ishlatiladi.

question_text: If it is sunny tomorrow, we _______ to the park.
explanation: Kelajakdagi real vaziyat (First Conditional) bo'lgani uchun 'if' qismida Present Simple, natija qismida 'will + fe'l' ishlatiladi.

question_text: If I had more time, I _______ read more books.
explanation: Hozirgi vaqtdagi xayoliy vaziyat (Second Conditional) uchun 'would' yordamchi fe'lidan foydalanamiz.

question_text: If you heat water to 100 degrees, it _______.
explanation: Bu ilmiy qonuniyat (Zero Conditional) bo'lgani uchun ikkala qismda ham Present Simple ishlatilishi kerak.

question_text: If I _______ you, I would take that job offer.
explanation: Bu xayoliy holat (Second Conditional). Past Simple qoidasiga ko'ra 'if' qismiga o'tgan zamon shakli qo'yiladi.

question_text: If we leave now, we _______ the bus on time.
explanation: Kelajakda amalga oshishi mumkin bo'lgan aniq vaziyat (First Conditional) bo'lgani uchun 'will + fe'l' shakli to'g'ri.

question_text: If he _______ harder yesterday, he would have finished his work.
explanation: O'tmishdagi afsus (Third Conditional) haqida gapirilmoqda, shuning uchun 'if' qismida Past Perfect (had + V3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: If I _______ a lottery ticket, I would be rich.
explanation: Xayoliy holat (Second Conditional) uchun 'if' qismida Past Simple shakli (bought) qo'llaniladi.

question_text: If she _______ me, I would have helped her.
explanation: Ushbu gap o'tmishdagi afsusni bildiradi (Third Conditional), shuning uchun 'if' qismi Past Perfect (had + V3) shaklida bo'lishi shart.

question_text: If they had arrived earlier, they _______ the train.
explanation: Third Conditional qoidasiga ko'ra, natija qismi 'would have + V3' shaklida bo'lishi kerak.

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: He said, 'I am tired.'
options: He said that he was tired.
He said that he is tired.
He said that I was tired.
He said that he am tired.
correct_answer: He said that he was tired.
explanation: According to Kartochka 2, the verb 'am' (Present Simple) shifts back to 'was' (Past Simple) when the reporting verb is 'said'.

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: She said, 'I live in Tashkent.'
options: She said that she lives in Tashkent.
She said that she lived in Tashkent.
She said that I lived in Tashkent.
She said that she had lived in Tashkent.
correct_answer: She said that she lived in Tashkent.
explanation: Based on Kartochka 2 and 3, 'live' changes to 'lived' (Past Simple) and 'I' changes to 'she'.

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: He said, 'I am reading.'
options: He said that he is reading.
He said that he was reading.
He said that he were reading.
correct_answer: He said that he was reading.
explanation: As per Kartochka 2, Present Continuous ('am reading') shifts to Past Continuous ('was reading').

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: She said, 'I have a car.'
options: She said that she has a car.
She said that she had a car.
She said that I had a car.
She said that she has had a car.
correct_answer: She said that she had a car.
explanation: According to Kartochka 2, the verb 'have' (Present Simple) shifts to the past form 'had'.

question_text: Complete the reported speech: He asked, 'Do you speak English?'
options: He asked that he spoke English.
He asked if I spoke English.
He asked that if I speak English.
He asked if I speak English.
correct_answer: He asked if I spoke English.
explanation: As per Kartochka 4, we do not use 'that' for questions, and we use 'if' for 'do' questions. Also, the tense shifts to Past Simple.

question_text: Complete the reported speech: She asked, 'Is he at home?'
options: She asked if he was at home.
She asked that he is at home.
She asked if he is at home.
She asked whether he has been at home.
correct_answer: She asked if he was at home.
explanation: According to Kartochka 4, we use 'if' for questions starting with 'is' and omit 'that'. The tense changes to Past Simple.

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: He said, 'I am working today.'
options: He said that he is working today.
He said that he was working today.
He said that he were working today.
He said that I was working today.
correct_answer: He said that he was working today.
explanation: Based on Kartochka 2, 'am working' (Present Continuous) shifts to 'was working' (Past Continuous) and 'I' becomes 'he' (Kartochka 3).

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: She said, 'I study hard.'
options: She said that she studies hard.
She said that she studied hard.
She said that I studied hard.
She said that she study hard.
correct_answer: She said that she studied hard.
explanation: Following Kartochka 2 and 3, 'I' changes to 'she' and 'study' (Present Simple) shifts to 'studied' (Past Simple).

question_text: Complete the reported speech: He asked, 'Does she like apples?'
options: He asked if she likes apples.
He asked that she liked apples.
He asked if she liked apples.
He asked whether she likes apples.
correct_answer: He asked if she liked apples.
explanation: Based on Kartochka 4, for 'does' questions we use 'if', remove 'that', and convert to a darak (affirmative) sentence with shifted tense ('liked').

question_text: Choose the correct reported speech for: She said, 'I am eating.'
options: She said that she was eating.
She said that I was eating.
She said that she is eating.
correct_answer: She said that she was eating.
explanation: Following Kartochka 2 and 3, 'am eating' (Present Continuous) shifts to 'was eating' (Past Continuous) and 'I' changes to 'she'.

question_text: Choose the correct relative pronoun: The girl ___ is singing a song is my sister.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, odamlar haqida gap ketganda 'who' so'zidan foydalanamiz.

question_text: Choose the correct relative pronoun: I lost the phone ___ I bought yesterday.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, narsalar haqida gap ketganda 'that' yoki 'which' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Choose the correct relative pronoun: The cat ___ is sleeping on the sofa is mine.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, hayvonlar haqida gap ketganda 'which' yoki 'that' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: The doctor ___ works at the hospital is very kind.
explanation: Gap inson (shifokor) haqida ketayotgani uchun Kartochka 2 ga muvofiq 'who' ishlatilishi kerak.

question_text: Identify the correct version: The laptop ___ is on the desk is broken.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, narsalar uchun 'which' yoki 'that' ishlatiladi. Bu yerda narsa (laptop) haqida gap ketyapti.

question_text: Which sentence is grammatically correct based on the usage of 'who'?
options: The man which I know is nice.
The man who I know is nice.
The man that I know is nice.
The man is who I know nice.
correct_answer: The man who I know is nice.
explanation: Kartochka 2 bo'yicha insonlar uchun 'who' so'zidan foydalanish to'g'ri hisoblanadi.

question_text: Choose the correct option: The computer ___ I use for my work is new.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, narsalar (kompyuter) haqida gap ketganda 'which' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Which sentence correctly uses the comma rule for extra information?
options: My teacher, who lives in Fergana, is very wise.
My teacher who lives in Fergana is very wise.
My teacher who, lives in Fergana, is very wise.
My teacher, who lives in Fergana is very wise.
correct_answer: My teacher, who lives in Fergana, is very wise.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, agar qo'shimcha ma'lumot shunchaki ortiqcha bo'lsa, u vergul bilan ajratiladi.

question_text: Choose the sentence where the information is essential (no comma needed):
options: My brother, who is a pilot, is coming home.
The man, who is wearing a hat, is my uncle.
The dog that is barking is aggressive.
My car, which is blue, is fast.
correct_answer: The dog that is barking is aggressive.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, agar ma'lumot aynan nima haqida ketayotganini aniqlash uchun shart bo'lsa, vergul qo'yilmaydi.

question_text: Which statement best describes the difference between 'who' and 'which'?
options: Who is for things, which is for people.
Who is for people, which is for things.
Both are used for animals only.
There is no difference in their usage.
correct_answer: Who is for people, which is for things.
explanation: Kartochka 2 va Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'who' insonlar uchun, 'which' esa narsalar va hayvonlar uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'They played football.'
explanation: Bu gap bitta ega va bitta kesimdan iborat hamda bitta mustaqil fikrni ifodalaydi. Shu sababli u sodda gap hisoblanadi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap turini aniqlang: 'The sun is shining.'
explanation: Ushbu gap bitta kesimga ega va bitta tugallangan fikrni ifodalaydi, shuning uchun bu sodda gapdir.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'He is smart, and he studies hard.'
explanation: Ushbu gapda ikkita mustaqil gap 'and' bog'lovchisi yordamida bog'langan. 'FANBOYS' bog'lovchilari ishlatilganda gap qo'shma gap bo'ladi.

question_text: Qaysi bog‘lovchi qo‘shma gap yasash uchun ishlatiladi?
explanation: Kartochkaga ko‘ra, 'and', 'but' va 'or' so‘zlari ikki sodda gapni bog‘lab, qo‘shma gap hosil qiladi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'I went to the park because the weather was nice.'
explanation: Gapda 'because' bog'lovchisi mavjud bo'lib, u asosiy va qaram qismlarni bog'lagan. Bu murakkab gapning belgisidir.

question_text: Quyidagi gap turini aniqlang: 'He plays football, or he plays tennis.'
explanation: Bu gap 'or' bog‘lovchisi orqali ikkita mustaqil fikrni birlashtirganligi sababli qo‘shma gap hisoblanadi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'If you want, I can help you.'
explanation: 'If' so'zi qaram qismni boshlab kelgan. Bitta mustaqil va bitta qaram qismdan iborat gaplar murakkab gaplardir.

question_text: Quyidagi gap turini aniqlang: 'She will go out if it stops raining.'
explanation: 'if' bog‘lovchisi orqali shartni ifodalovchi bo‘ysunuvchi qismga ega bo‘lgani uchun bu murakkab gapdir.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'I was hungry, so I ate an apple.'
explanation: 'so' bog'lovchisi 'FANBOYS' ro'yxatiga kiradi. U ikki mustaqil fikrni bog'lagani uchun bu qo'shma gapdir.

question_text: Quyidagi gapda qaysi turdagi bog‘lovchi ishlatilgan: 'I wanted to run, but I was tired.'
explanation: 'but' so‘zi 'and', 'or' kabi ikkita mustaqil sodda gapni birlashtirib, qo‘shma gap hosil qiladi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'Although it was cold, we went swimming.'
explanation: 'Although' bog'lovchisi gapning qaram qismini boshlab kelgan. Qaram qism to'liq ma'no bermaydi, shuning uchun bu murakkab gap.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qanday gap turiga kiradi: 'They arrived home when the movie started.'
explanation: Ushbu gap 'when' bog‘lovchisi yordamida asosiy gapga qo‘shimcha qism qo‘shgani uchun murakkab gap sanaladi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'She likes tea or she likes coffee.'
explanation: 'or' bog'lovchisi 'FANBOYS' guruhiga kiradi. U teng huquqli ikki sodda gapni bog'lab, qo'shma gap hosil qilgan.

question_text: Quyidagi gapni to‘ldiring: 'He is tired, ___ he is still working.' (Qo‘shma gap yasang)
explanation: Qo‘shma gap hosil qilish uchun 'but' bog‘lovchisi mos keladi, chunki u ikkita mustaqil gapni bog‘laydi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'When the bell rang, the students left the class.'
explanation: 'When' bog'lovchisi orqali qaram qism hosil qilingan. Asosiy va qaram qism birikmasi murakkab gapni tashkil etadi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap nima uchun murakkab gap hisoblanadi: 'I ate dinner because I was hungry.'
options: U ikkita teng mustaqil fikrga ega
U 'because' orqali sababni ko‘rsatuvchi bo‘ysunuvchi qismga ega
correct_answer: U 'because' orqali sababni ko‘rsatuvchi bo‘ysunuvchi qismga ega
explanation: Murakkab gaplar sabab, shart yoki vaqt kabi qo‘shimcha ma’nolarni 'because', 'if', 'when' kabi bog‘lovchilar bilan ifodalaydi.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'The sun rises in the east.'
explanation: Gapda hech qanday bog'lovchi yo'q va u bitta mustaqil fikrni ifodalaydigan bitta ega va kesimga ega sodda gapdir.

question_text: Quyidagilardan qaysi biri murakkab gap emas?
options: Although it is late, I will study.
He works hard because he wants success.
He studied, and he passed the exam.
If you call me, I will come.
correct_answer: He studied, and he passed the exam.
explanation: Ushbu gap 'and' bog‘lovchisi bilan bog‘langan ikkita mustaqil sodda gapdan iborat bo‘lib, u qo‘shma gapdir, murakkab gap emas.

question_text: Quyidagi gap qaysi turga kiradi? 'He worked hard, yet he failed the test.'
explanation: 'yet' bog'lovchisi 'FANBOYS' ro'yxatida mavjud. U ikki mustaqil sodda gapni bog'lab, qo'shma gap yaratgan.

question_text: Qaysi gapda 'because' bog‘lovchisi to‘g‘ri qo‘llanilgan va u murakkab gap hisoblanadi?
options: She is happy, because she won.
Because she won, she is happy.
She is happy because she won.
explanation: Murakkab gaplarda 'because' bog‘lovchisi asosiy gapga sababni qo‘shadi va u gapning istalgan joyida bo‘ysunuvchi qismni bog‘lab kela oladi.

question_text: The cat ___ in the garden.
explanation: Ega 'The cat' birlikda bo'lgani uchun, 1-kartochkaga ko'ra fe'lga '-s' qo'shimchasi qo'shiladi.

question_text: She ________ a new car every year.
explanation: Ega 'she' (birlik) bo'lgani uchun, Oltin Qoidaga ko'ra fe'lga -s qo'shimchasi qo'shiladi.

question_text: They ___ milk every morning.
explanation: Ega 'They' ko'plikda bo'lgani uchun, 1-kartochkaga ko'ra fe'l o'z holicha qoladi.

question_text: They ________ to the cinema on Fridays.
explanation: Ega 'they' (ko'plik) bo'lgani uchun, Oltin Qoidaga ko'ra fe'l o'z holicha (qo'shimchasiz) qoladi.

question_text: Sarah and Mike ___ to the cinema on Fridays.
explanation: 2-kartochkaga ko'ra, 'and' bilan bog'langan ikkita ega ko'plik hisoblanadi, shuning uchun fe'lga qo'shimcha qo'shilmaydi.

question_text: I ________ English very well.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'I' olmoshi har doim ko'plikdagi fe'l bilan ishlatiladi.

question_text: Each boy ___ a task.
explanation: 3-kartochkaga ko'ra, 'each' so'zi egani birlik deb ko'rsatadi, shuning uchun fe'l birlik shaklida bo'ladi.

question_text: Sara and Tom ________ in the garden.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'and' bilan bog'langan egalar ko'plik hisoblanadi, shuning uchun fe'lga -s qo'shilmaydi.

question_text: The box of old books ___ under the table.
explanation: 4-kartochkaga ko'ra, asosiy ega 'box' birlikda, shuning uchun fe'lga '-s' qo'shiladi. 'Books' so'zi bizni chalg'itmasligi kerak.

question_text: My brother ________ his homework in the evening.
explanation: Ega 'my brother' (birlik) bo'lgani uchun, Oltin Qoidaga muvofiq fe'lga -es (does) qo'shimchasi qo'shiladi.

question_text: Every student ___ a notebook.
explanation: 3-kartochkaga ko'ra, 'every' so'zi kelganda fe'lga '-s' qo'shiladi, chunki u guruhni birlik deb ko'rsatadi.

question_text: The cat and the dog ________ milk.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga muvofiq, 'and' bilan bog'langan egalar ko'plikni anglatadi, shuning uchun fe'l o'z holicha qoladi.

question_text: My brother and my sister ___ English well.
explanation: 2-kartochkaga ko'ra, 'and' bilan bog'langan ikkita shaxs ko'plikni anglatadi, shuning uchun fe'l o'z holicha qoladi.

question_text: You ________ very fast.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'you' olmoshi har doim ko'plikdagi fe'l bilan ishlatiladi.

question_text: The smell of fresh flowers ___ wonderful.
explanation: 4-kartochkaga ko'ra, gapning asosiy egasi 'smell' birlikda, shuning uchun biz birlikdagi fe'lni tanlaymiz.

question_text: Neither the manager nor the workers ________ here now.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, 'nor' bilan bog'langanda fe'l eng yaqin egaga ('workers' - ko'plik) qarab moslashadi.

question_text: Each of the apples ___ sweet.
explanation: 3-kartochkaga ko'ra, 'each' so'zi guruhni birlik deb ko'rsatadi, shuning uchun fe'lga '-s' qo'shiladi.

question_text: Either the students or the teacher ________ the document.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, 'or' bilan bog'langanda fe'l eng yaqin egaga ('teacher' - birlik) qarab moslashadi va -s qo'shiladi.

question_text: The key to the secret doors ___ here.
explanation: 4-kartochkaga ko'ra, 'doors' so'ziga qaramay, asosiy ega 'key' birlikda bo'lgani uchun fe'lga '-s' qo'shiladi.

question_text: Neither the boys nor my friend ________ the answer.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, 'nor' bilan bog'langanda fe'l eng yaqin egaga ('my friend' - birlik) qarab moslashadi va -s qo'shiladi.

question_text: I take an umbrella with me ______ it is raining outside.
explanation: Ushbu gapda ish-harakatning sababi tushuntirilmoqda. 'Because' bog'lovchisi sabab ergash gaplarini kiritish uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: I will wait here ______ the bus comes.
explanation: Gapda harakat qachon sodir bo'lishini bildirish uchun vaqt bog'lovchisi 'when' mos keladi.

question_text: ______ he was tired, he finished his homework.
explanation: Gapda charchagan bo'lsa ham ishni bajargani aytilmoqda, ya'ni ikki qism bir-biriga zid. Zidlikni bildirish uchun 'although' ishlatiladi.

question_text: She went to bed early ______ she was very tired.
explanation: Uning erta uxlashiga sabab charchagani bo'lgani uchun, sabab bog'lovchisi 'because' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Please turn off the lights ______ you leave the room.
explanation: Gap ish-harakat qachon sodir bo'lishi kerakligini (vaqtni) ko'rsatmoqda, shuning uchun 'when' to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: He saved money ______ he could buy a new bike.
explanation: Maqsadni ifodalash uchun 'so that' bog'lovchisi ishlatiladi va undan keyin 'could' zamoni qo'llangan.

question_text: He went to the library ______ borrow some books.
explanation: Maqsadni bildirish uchun 'to' bog'lovchisidan foydalanamiz, undan keyin esa fe'lning o'zi keladi.

question_text: ______ it was raining, they played football in the garden.
explanation: Yomg'ir yog'ishi va futbol o'ynash kutilmagan natija bo'lgani uchun, zidlik bog'lovchisi 'although' tanlanadi.

question_text: ______ it is getting dark, we should head back home.
explanation: 'Since' sabab ergash gaplarini kiritishda ishlatiladi va bu yerda kech bo'layotgani sababli uyga qaytish kerakligini bildiradi.

question_text: I cleaned the kitchen ______ my mother comes back home.
explanation: Ish-harakat qachon bajarilganini ko'rsatish uchun vaqt bog'lovchisi 'before' to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: I exercised hard ______ I could stay fit.
explanation: Maqsadni ko'rsatish uchun 'so that' ishlatiladi va undan keyin gap (I could stay fit) kelgan.

question_text: He practiced English every day ______ improve his speaking skills.
explanation: Maqsadni ifodalash uchun 'to' ishlatiladi va undan keyin fe'lning o'zi keladi.

question_text: They played football ______ it started to rain.
explanation: Ish-harakat qancha vaqtgacha davom etganini ko'rsatish uchun vaqt bog'lovchisi 'until' ishlatiladi.

question_text: ______ she studied well, she didn't get the highest score.
explanation: Garchi u yaxshi o'qisa ham yuqori ball ololmagani kutilmagan natija bo'lgani uchun, zidlik bog'lovchisi 'even though' ishlatiladi.

question_text: ______ having a lot of work, she helped me with my project.
explanation: 'Despite' zidlikni bildiradi va 'garchi... bo'lsa ham' ma'nosida ishlatiladi.

question_text: He left the meeting early ______ he had an urgent task.
explanation: Uchrashuvdan erta ketishining sababi shoshilinch ish bo'lgani uchun, sabab bog'lovchisi 'since' mos keladi.

question_text: ______ the alarm clock rang, he didn't wake up.
explanation: Alarm jiringlasa ham uyg'onmagani zidlikni bildiradi. 'Even though' zidlik ergash gaplarida qo'llaniladi.

question_text: ______ his hard work, he couldn't finish the project on time.
explanation: Zidlikni ifodalash uchun berilgan variantlar ichida 'despite' kutilmagan natijani ko'rsatuvchi to'g'ri bog'lovchi hisoblanadi.

question_text: She saves money ______ she can travel to Europe next summer.
explanation: Maqsadni ifodalashda 'so that' dan keyin gap (she can travel) keladi. 'To' dan keyin esa fe'l kelishi kerak edi.

question_text: I will prepare the report ______ the boss arrives.
explanation: Vaqt ergash gaplarida ish-harakat qachon sodir bo'lishini ko'rsatish uchun 'while' bog'lovchisi mos keladi.

question_text: Choose the correct option: I think ______ he is a good teacher.
explanation: We use 'that' to confirm a fact or opinion after verbs like 'think'. According to Card 2, 'that' connects the noun clause.

question_text: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence: He said _______ he was hungry.
explanation: According to Card 2, we use 'that' to express a fact or opinion, and it can sometimes be omitted. It is the appropriate connector for this statement.

question_text: Select the correct word order: Can you tell me where ______?
explanation: According to Card 3, when we put a question inside another sentence, the word order changes to a statement structure, so 'where he is' is correct.

question_text: Complete the sentence: I want to know _______ she is coming to the party.
explanation: According to Card 4, we use 'if' or 'whether' for yes/no questions to express 'mi' (the 'mi' suffix) within a sentence.

question_text: Complete the sentence: I don't know ______ she will arrive on time or not.
explanation: Card 4 states that we use 'if' or 'whether' for yes/no questions to express 'mi?' meaning.

question_text: Select the correct sentence structure for the noun clause: I don't know _______.
explanation: As stated in Card 3, in wh-questions, the word order must be like a normal statement, meaning the verb comes after the subject.

question_text: Which sentence is grammatically correct based on the rules?
options: He said that he is busy.
I think if he is good.
She knows that he does lives there.
correct_answer: He said that he is busy.
explanation: According to Card 2, 'that' is used to report a statement or fact after verbs like 'said'.

question_text: Choose the word that can be omitted in this sentence: I think that he is a good doctor.
explanation: Card 2 explains that the 'that' connector is optional and can be left out without changing the meaning of the sentence.

question_text: Choose the best way to complete the sentence: Do you know ______ they finished the report?
explanation: Since the question expects a yes/no answer (did they finish or not?), we use 'if' as described in Card 4.

question_text: Fill in the blank: The teacher asked me _______ I had finished my homework.
explanation: Card 4 states that 'whether' (like 'if') is used for yes/no questions, effectively serving as the 'mi' particle in indirect speech.

question_text: Choose the correct word order for this sentence: I wonder where ______.
explanation: Card 3 explains that auxiliary verbs like 'did' disappear and the sentence takes the form of a statement when used inside another sentence.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence:
options: She asked what is the time.
She asked what the time is.
She asked that the time is.
She asked if the time what is.
correct_answer: She asked what the time is.
explanation: Card 3 specifies that wh-questions must follow a normal statement word order, where the subject comes before the verb.

question_text: Which option can be omitted in the sentence: 'She believes (______) he is honest'?
explanation: According to Card 2, 'that' can often be dropped to make the sentence flow better in English.

question_text: Which connector is best to use if you are unsure about a 'yes/no' situation?
explanation: Card 4 explains that 'if' is used to turn yes/no questions into noun clauses, functioning like the 'mi' suffix.

question_text: Complete: I am not sure ______ he wants to join us.
explanation: When we express uncertainty or need a yes/no answer, we use 'if' or 'whether' (Card 4).

question_text: Identify the noun clause in this sentence: 'I wonder why he left so early.'
explanation: Card 1 defines a noun clause as a group of words acting as a noun, and Card 3 confirms that wh-words start these clauses.

question_text: Identify the correct structure: I know ______.
explanation: Based on Card 3, the auxiliary verb is removed and the subject comes before the verb to create a statement structure.

question_text: Choose the correct structure: I am not sure _______.
explanation: Card 3 requires the subject ('it') to come before the verb ('is') in a noun clause starting with a wh-word ('what time').

question_text: Which of the following is correct regarding formal usage?
explanation: Card 4 clearly states that 'whether' is more formal than 'if' when dealing with yes/no questions.

question_text: Which of the following is correct for expressing a fact?
options: I know if he is tired.
I know whether he is tired.
I know that he is tired.
I know what he is tired.
correct_answer: I know that he is tired.
explanation: Card 2 notes that 'that' is used for facts or opinions, whereas 'if/whether' (Cards 1 and 4) are used for yes/no questions.

question_text: I ________ drink juice than soda.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'would rather' tanlov qilishda ishlatiladi va o'z istagingizni bildiradi.

question_text: I ________ drink juice than soda.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'would rather' o'z istagini bildirish va tanlov qilish uchun ishlatiladi hamda '... qilgan bo'lardim' deb tarjima qilinadi.

question_text: I wish I ________ the truth right now.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, hozirgi holatdan norozilik bildirilsa, 'I wish' dan keyin Past Simple ishlatiladi.

question_text: I wish I ________ more free time today.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, hozirgi holatdan norozilik bildirilganda 'I wish' dan keyin fe'l Past Simple zamonida (had) bo'lishi kerak.

question_text: ________ I were more patient!
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'If only' qaniydi ma'nosida his-tuyg'uni kuchaytirish uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: ________ I could speak Spanish fluently!
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'If only' 'Qaniydi...' deb tarjima qilinadi va afsuslanishni yanada ta'sirliroq ko'rsatish uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: I would rather ________ a book than watch TV.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'would rather' dan keyin fe'l hech qanday qo'shimchasiz, oddiy ko'rinishda keladi.

question_text: I would rather ________ a book than watch TV.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'would rather' dan keyin fe'l hech qanday qo'shimchasiz, oddiy ko'rinishda kelishi shart.

question_text: I regret not buying that car. I wish I ________ it.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, o'tgan zamonda qilingan ishdan afsuslanganda 'had + V3' (Past Perfect) ishlatiladi.

question_text: I feel so tired. I wish I ________ to bed earlier last night.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, o'tmishda qilingan ishdan afsuslanganda 'wish' dan keyin Past Perfect (had + V3) zamoni ishlatiladi.

question_text: The room is too dark. If only it ________ brighter in here.
explanation: Kartochka 1 va 2 ga ko'ra, hozirgi holatdan norozilik 'I wish' yoki 'If only' bilan bir xil qoida bo'yicha (Past Simple) yasaladi.

question_text: If only I ________ younger! I would travel the world.
explanation: Kartochka 1 va 2 ga muvofiq, hozirgi holatdan norozilik bildirilganda Past Simple (fe'lning o'tgan zamon shakli) ishlatiladi.

question_text: I ________ walk to work than take the bus.
explanation: Tanlovni ifodalovchi birikma 'would rather' hisoblanadi.

question_text: I wish I ________ the train yesterday, but I missed it.
explanation: Gap o'tmishdagi (yesterday) voqeani anglatadi, shuning uchun Kartochka 4 bo'yicha Past Perfect (had + V3) ishlatilishi kerak.

question_text: I wish I ________ to the party last night; it was boring.
explanation: O'tmishda sodir bo'lgan voqeadan afsuslanish uchun Kartochka 4 ga binoan Past Perfect ishlatiladi.

question_text: She ________ stay at home than go to the party.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, tanlovni bildirish uchun 'would rather' ishlatiladi.

question_text: She feels sick today. She wishes she ________ so much candy yesterday.
explanation: Kecha (o'tmishda) yegani uchun afsuslanyapti, shuning uchun Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra Past Perfect ishlatiladi.

question_text: I wish I ________ my keys. I can't find them anywhere now.
explanation: Kalitlar o'tmishda yo'qolgan va hozir bundan afsuslanilmoqda. Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, o'tmish afsusi uchun Past Perfect ishlatiladi.

question_text: I wish I ________ how to play the piano, but I never learned.
explanation: Gap hozirgi paytda pianino chalishni bilmaslikdan afsuslanishni anglatadi, shuning uchun Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra Past Simple ishlatiladi.

question_text: If only I ________ that he was lying to me last week!
explanation: Gap o'tgan hafta (last week) sodir bo'lgan ish haqida. O'tmishdagi afsus uchun Kartochka 4 ga binoan Past Perfect qo'llaniladi.

question_text: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence: _________ in the library, the students were silent.
explanation: Ishni bajarayotgan subyekt (students) bo'lgani uchun aktiv ma'noda -ing shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ a cup of tea, she sat down on the sofa.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, ikkita ish bir vaqtda sodir bo'lganda va ega bir xil bo'lsa, -ing shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: The cake _________ by my mother is delicious.
explanation: Tort o'z-o'zidan pishmaydi, u onam tomonidan pishirilgan (passiv ma'noda). Shuning uchun fe'lning 3-shakli -ed ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ by a famous architect, the building is very beautiful.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, gap passiv (majhul) ma'noda bo'lsa, fe'lning 3-shakli (V3/ed) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Select the correct option: _________ to the music, she felt happy.
explanation: Musiqani tinglayotgan ham, baxtli bo'lgan ham 'she' (egasi bir xil). Aktiv harakat uchun -ing ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ my work, I went to bed.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, bir ish ikkinchisidan oldin tugagan bo'lsa, 'Having + V3' shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: Choose the correct form: The window _________ in the storm was broken.
explanation: Deraza bo'ron tomonidan sindirilgan (passiv ma'no). Shuning uchun fe'lning 3-shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ in a small village, he knows everyone there.
explanation: Harakat ayni vaqtda sodir bo'layotgani uchun -ing shakli (Kartochka 1) to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: _________ very tired, I decided to go home early.
explanation: Bu yerda sabab keltirilmoqda (charchaganim uchun). Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, sababni qisqartirish uchun -ing shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ the window, I felt a cold breeze.
explanation: Derazani ochish va sovuqni his qilish bir vaqtda sodir bo'lgani uchun -ing shakli (Kartochka 1) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Which sentence follows the rule about the subject (egasi bir xil)?
options: Walking to the park, the trees were beautiful.
Walking to the park, I saw beautiful trees.
Walking to the park, it was sunny.
Walking to the park, the flowers smelled nice.
correct_answer: Walking to the park, I saw beautiful trees.
explanation: Qisqartirilgan qismdagi 'walking' harakatini 'I' (men) bajarayapman. Boshqa variantlarda egasi to'g'ri mos kelmagan.

question_text: ____ all the food, the guests left the party.
explanation: Ovqatni yeb bo'lgandan so'ng mehmonlar ketgani uchun tugallangan ishni bildiruvchi 'Having + V3' (Kartochka 3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Choose the correct option: The letter _________ on the table contains important news.
explanation: Xat stol ustiga qo'yilgan (passiv holat). Shuning uchun 'left' (V3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ in the dark, the mysterious box looked scary.
explanation: Quti 'ko'rilgan' (passiv ma'no) bo'lgani uchun Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra fe'lning 3-shakli (V3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: _________ a lot of water, the plants grew quickly.
explanation: O'simliklarga suv berilgan (passiv). Shuning uchun fe'lning 3-shakli -ed/-en (Given) ishlatiladi.

question_text: ____ her mistakes, she corrected them immediately.
explanation: Xatolarni anglash va ularni tuzatish bir vaqtda sodir bo'layotgani uchun -ing shakli (Kartochka 1) to'g'ri keladi.

question_text: Select the sentence where the participle is used correctly to show a reason:
options: Not knowing the way, the map was useful.
Not knowing the way, I got lost.
Not knowing the way, the car stopped.
Not knowing the way, it was difficult.
correct_answer: Not knowing the way, I got lost.
explanation: Yo'lni bilmayotgan shaxs 'I' (men) bo'lishi kerak. Faqat shu variantda egasi va harakat mantiqan mos keladi.

question_text: ____ the letter, he realized he had made a mistake.
explanation: Maktubni o'qib bo'lgandan so'ng xato qilganini anglagani uchun, avval tugagan ishni bildiruvchi 'Having + V3' (Kartochka 3) ishlatiladi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: _________ by the teacher, the students understood the lesson better.
explanation: O'quvchilar o'qituvchi tomonidan yordam berilgan holatda (passiv). Shuning uchun fe'lning 3-shakli ishlatiladi.

question_text: Which sentence follows the 'Ega mosligi' rule (Kartochka 4)?
options: Walking down the street, the sun was shining.
Walking down the street, I saw a cat.
Walking down the street, the trees were tall.
Walking down the street, the weather was nice.
correct_answer: Walking down the street, I saw a cat.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, asosiy gapning egasi qisqartirilgan harakatni bajargan shaxs bo'lishi kerak. Faqat shu variantda 'men' ko'chada yurgan shaxs hisoblanaman.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence using inversion: ___________ such a beautiful flower.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'Rarely' bilan boshlangan gaplarda inversiya (yordamchi fe'lning egadan oldinga o'tishi) talab qilinadi.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence using inversion with 'Never':
options: Never I have seen such a performance.
Never have I seen such a performance.
Never I seen such a performance.
I have never seen such a performance.
correct_answer: Never have I seen such a performance.
explanation: According to Card 1 and 2, when a sentence starts with 'Never', we must use the inversion form (auxiliary verb before the subject), similar to a question.

question_text: Complete the sentence: Never ___________ so angry.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga binoan, 'Never' inkor so'zi bilan boshlangan gaplarda inversiya qilinadi va yordamchi fe'l egadan oldinga chiqadi.

question_text: Complete the sentence: 'Seldom ___ to the cinema.'
explanation: Per Card 2, 'Seldom' triggers inversion. We use the auxiliary 'does' before the subject 'he', and the main verb returns to its base form 'go'.

question_text: Identify the correct inversion structure: ___________ do I go to the cinema.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'Seldom' kabi inkor ma'noli so'zlar gap boshida inversiyani talab qiladi.

question_text: Which sentence correctly uses 'Not only... but also'?
options: Not only he sings, but also he dances.
Not only does he sing, but also he dances.
Not only does he sing, but also dances he.
Not he only sings, but also he dances.
correct_answer: Not only does he sing, but also he dances.
explanation: Card 4 states that when 'Not only' starts a sentence, inversion occurs in the first part, while the second part remains unchanged.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence: ___________ realize the danger I was in.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga binoan, 'Little' (xabar yo'qligini bildirib) gap boshida kelganda inversiya qilinishi shart.

question_text: Identify the inverted sentence:
explanation: Card 2 explains that words like 'Rarely' require the auxiliary verb to be placed before the subject.

question_text: Select the correct option: ___________ she finish her work, but she also helped me.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, 'Not only' bilan boshlangan gaplarda inversiya qilinishi va 'but also' qismi saqlanishi kerak.

question_text: Choose the correct inversion for: 'Only after the rain stopped did we go out.'
options: Only after the rain stopped we went out.
Only after did the rain stop we went out.
Only after the rain stopped did we go out.
Only after the rain did stop we went out.
correct_answer: Only after the rain stopped did we go out.
explanation: Per Card 3, the inversion happens in the main part of the sentence ('did we go out'), not in the 'Only after...' phrase.

question_text: Complete the sentence: Only after the meeting ___________ able to speak.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga binoan, 'Only after' iborasi bilan boshlangan gapning asosiy qismida inversiya yuz beradi.

question_text: Rewrite the sentence 'He hardly eats meat' using inversion correctly:
explanation: As mentioned in Card 2, 'Hardly' is an inkor (negative) word, so we must invert the subject and auxiliary verb.

question_text: Choose the correct version: ___________ understand the complexity of the task.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga muvofiq, 'Only when' iborasi bilan boshlangan gapda asosiy qismda yordamchi fe'l egadan oldinga o'tib, inversiya hosil bo'ladi.

question_text: Complete: 'Only when I received the letter ___ the truth.'
explanation: Card 3 states that inversion occurs in the main clause after 'Only when'. We use 'did' because the action is in the past.

question_text: Identify the grammatically correct sentence using inversion:
options: Hardly he had arrived when it started to rain.
Hardly had he arrived when it started to rain.
Hardly he did arrive when it started to rain.
Hardly did he arrive when it started to rain.
correct_answer: Hardly had he arrived when it started to rain.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'Hardly' inkor so'zi bilan boshlanganda inversiya qilinadi, bu yerda 'had' yordamchi fe'li ega 'he'dan oldinga o'tadi.

question_text: Choose the correct structure:
options: Not only she is smart, but also she is kind.
Not only is she smart, but also she is kind.
Not only is she smart, but she is also kind.
Not only she is smart, but she is also kind.
correct_answer: Not only is she smart, but she is also kind.
explanation: According to Card 4, inversion ('is she') is required in the first part of the 'Not only' structure.

question_text: Which sentence is correct according to the inversion rules?
options: Only in this way does he solve the problem.
Only in this way he solves the problem.
Only in this way he does solve the problem.
Only in this way did he solves the problem.
correct_answer: Only in this way does he solve the problem.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'Only in this way' iborasi inversiyani talab qiladi; 'does' yordamchi fe'li egadan oldin kelishi to'g'ri shakldir.

question_text: Identify the grammatically correct sentence:
options: Only then I realized my mistake.
Only then did I realize my mistake.
Only then I did realize my mistake.
Only then realized I my mistake.
correct_answer: Only then did I realize my mistake.
explanation: Card 3 dictates that after 'Only then', the main clause must follow an inverted word order (auxiliary verb before subject).

question_text: Select the correct sentence using 'Not only' structure:
options: Not only did he sing, he also danced.
Not only he sang, but also he danced.
Not only did he sing, but he also danced.
Not only he did sing, but also he danced.
correct_answer: Not only did he sing, but he also danced.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga binoan, 'Not only' inversiyani (did he) va 'but also' bog'lovchisini talab qiladi.

question_text: Which of the following is correct?
options: Seldom she has been to such places.
Seldom has she been to such places.
She seldom has been to such places.
Seldom she does been to such places.
correct_answer: Seldom has she been to such places.
explanation: According to Card 2, 'Seldom' requires inversion. Here, the auxiliary 'has' moves before the subject 'she'.

question_text: Choose the correct verb: I ______ a lot of friends in this city.
explanation: The verb 'have' indicates possession, which fits the context of owning or having something, in this case, friends.

question_text: Complete the sentence: Every morning, I ______ to work by bus.
explanation: The verb 'go' is used to express movement to a destination, which is appropriate for traveling to work.

question_text: Select the verb for the blank: She ______ a delicious cake for her birthday.
explanation: 'Make' is the correct verb here because it means to create or prepare something, like a cake.

question_text: Identify the correct verb: Please ______ me your book, I want to read it.
explanation: 'Give' is the correct verb used when transferring an object from one person to another.

question_text: Fill in the blank: My brother ______ how to speak English very well.
explanation: 'Know' is a state verb used to describe information or knowledge that someone possesses.

question_text: Choose the best verb: Can you ______ that blue pen from the table for me?
explanation: 'Take' is used when moving an object from one place to another or removing it from a location.

question_text: Complete the sentence: I ______ my daily exercises every evening.
explanation: 'Do' is used to refer to performing activities or tasks, such as exercises or homework.

question_text: Choose the correct option: He ______ a doctor at the local hospital.
explanation: 'Is' is a form of 'be', which is used to describe a person's state or role (a doctor).

question_text: Select the correct verb for this situation: Did you ______ the email I sent you yesterday?
explanation: 'Get' is used to indicate receiving information or an item, which fits the context of receiving an email.

question_text: Fill in the blank: They ______ a meeting to discuss the new project tomorrow.
explanation: In this context, 'have' is used to denote an experience or a scheduled event, similar to possessing something.

question_text: Frazeologik fe'llarni tushunish bo'yicha qaysi fikr to'g'ri?
options: Ularni doimo so'zma-so'z tarjima qilish kerak
Ular oddiy fe'l va bog'lovchidan hosil bo'ladi
Frazeologik fe'llar faqat bitta ma'noga ega
correct_answer: Ular oddiy fe'l va bog'lovchidan hosil bo'ladi
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, frazeologik fe'l oddiy fe'lga bog'lovchi qo'shilishi orqali hosil bo'lgan yangi ma'noli iboradir.

question_text: Quyidagi gaplardan qaysi biri 'look after' (g'amxo'rlik qilmoq) iborasi uchun to'g'ri?
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, ayrim fe'llarni ajratib bo'lmaydi va to'ldiruvchi har doim iboradan keyin kelishi kerak.

question_text: Agar to'ldiruvchi 'it' kishi olmoshi bo'lsa, 'put on' (kiymoq) fe'li bilan qanday ishlatiladi?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, agar to'ldiruvchi kishi olmoshi (it, me, him va h.k.) bo'lsa, u majburan iboraning o'rtasiga qo'yiladi.

question_text: Frazeologik fe'llar haqida qaysi qoida noto'g'ri?
options: Bitta fe'l turli bog'lovchilar bilan har xil ma'no berishi mumkin
Ularning ma'nosini kontekstdan ajratib olish kerak
Frazeologik fe'llarni har doim so'zma-so'z tarjima qilish mumkin
correct_answer: Frazeologik fe'llarni har doim so'zma-so'z tarjima qilish mumkin
explanation: Kartochka 1 va 4 ga ko'ra, frazeologik fe'llarni so'zma-so'z tarjima qilib bo'lmaydi, chunki ular yangi ma'no kasb etadi.

question_text: Quyidagi gaplardan qaysi biri 'take off' (echmoq) iborasi uchun to'g'ri?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, ajraluvchi iboralarda to'ldiruvchi so'z iboraning o'rtasiga ham, oxiriga ham kela oladi.

question_text: Kontekstga ko'ra 'get' fe'lining o'zgarishi haqida nima deyish mumkin?
options: 'Get' har doim bir xil ma'noni anglatadi
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, bitta fe'l turli qo'shimchalar (bog'lovchilar) bilan butunlay boshqa ma'no berishi mumkin.

question_text: Qaysi gapda 'give up' (voz kechmoq) iborasi to'g'ri qo'llangan?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'give up' kabi ajraluvchi iboralarda ot to'ldiruvchi o'rtaga ham, oxiriga ham kela oladi.

question_text: Kishi olmoshlari (me, him, her, it, them) frazeologik fe'llarda qanday o'ringa ega?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, agar to'ldiruvchi kishi olmoshi bo'lsa, u majburan fe'l va bog'lovchi o'rtasiga qo'yiladi.

question_text: Agar 'pick up' (ko'tarib olmoq) ajraluvchi ibora bo'lsa, 'them' olmoshi bilan qanday yoziladi?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'them' kishi olmoshi bo'lgani uchun u majburan iboraning o'rtasiga qo'yilishi shart.

question_text: Ajralmas iboralar haqida qaysi tasdiq to'g'ri?
options: Ularni ajratish mumkin, agar olmosh ishlatsak
To'ldiruvchi doimo iboradan keyin kelishi shart
Ularni ajratish mumkin, agar ot ishlatsak
correct_answer: To'ldiruvchi doimo iboradan keyin kelishi shart
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, ajralmas iboralarda fe'l va bog'lovchini aslo ajratib bo'lmaydi va to'ldiruvchi har doim iboradan keyin keladi.

question_text: 'Happy' (baxtli) so'zini inkor ma'nosiga (baxtsiz) o'tkazish uchun qaysi prefixdan foydalanamiz?
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'un-' qo'shimchasi so'zni inkor ma'nosiga o'tkazadi. 'Un' + 'happy' = 'Unhappy' so'zi hosil bo'ladi.

question_text: Quyidagilardan qaysi biri so'zning oxiriga qo'shilib, uning so'z turkumini o'zgartiruvchi suffix hisoblanadi?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, '-able' so'zning oxiriga qo'shiladigan suffix bo'lib, u so'z turkumini o'zgartirishga xizmat qiladi.

question_text: So'zning boshiga qo'shilib, uning ma'nosini aksincha qiluvchi qism nima deb ataladi?
explanation: Kartochka 1 va 2 da aytilishicha, so'zning boshiga qo'shiladigan va ma'noni o'zgartiradigan qism 'prefix' deb ataladi.

question_text: 'Clean' (tozalamoq) fe'lidan 'tozalovchi' (kasb) ma'nosini yasash uchun qaysi suffixdan foydalanamiz?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga binoan, '-er' qo'shimchasi fe'ldan kasb-hunar bildiruvchi so'z yasash uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: 'Agree' (rozi bo'lmoq) so'zini inkor ma'nosiga (rozi bo'lmaslik) o'tkazish uchun qaysi prefixdan foydalanamiz?
explanation: Kartochka 2 da keltirilgan misollarga ko'ra, 'dis-' qo'shimchasi 'dislike' so'zida bo'lgani kabi inkor ma'nosini ifodalaydi.

question_text: Quyidagi so'zlardan qaysi biri suffix yordamida sifatga aylantirilgan?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, '-able' qo'shimchasi 'Comfort' so'ziga qo'shilib, uni sifat darajasiga (Comfortable) o'tkazadi.

question_text: 'Possible' (imkonli) so'zini inkor ma'nosiga (imkonsiz) o'tkazish uchun qaysi prefix ishlatiladi?
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'Impossible' so'zi inkor ma'nosini beradi. Garchi misolda 'im-' ko'rinishida bo'lsa ham, bu 'in-' prefixining o'zgarishidir.

question_text: 'Move' (harakatlanmoq) so'ziga suffix qo'shib, 'harakat' (ot) so'zini yasash uchun qaysi variant to'g'ri?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, '-ment' qo'shimchasi so'zning oxiriga qo'shilib, uning turkumini o'zgartiradi.

question_text: Agar 'Play' (o'ynamoq) o'zak so'z bo'lsa, 'o'yinchi' ma'nosini ifodalash uchun qaysi qo'shimcha turidan foydalanamiz?
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, '-er' kabi suffixlar fe'ldan kasb yoki shaxs (o'yinchi - player) yasash uchun so'z oxiriga qo'shiladi.

question_text: Quyidagi qaysi juftlikda 'prefix' qo'llanilgan?
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'un-' prefixi so'z boshiga qo'shiladi. Qolgan variantlar (-er, -able) suffix hisoblanadi.

question_text: Qaysi juftlik sinonimlarga misol bo'la oladi?
explanation: Sinonimlar ma'nosi bir-biriga juda yaqin bo'lgan so'zlardir. 'Happy' va 'Glad' so'zlari ikkisi ham 'xursand' degan ma'noni anglatadi.

question_text: 'Cold' (sovuq) so'zining antonimi qaysi?
explanation: Antonimlar qarama-qarshi ma'noli so'zlardir. 'Hot' (issiq) so'zi 'Cold' (sovuq) so'zining qarama-qarshisidir.

question_text: 'Small' so'ziga sinonim bo'la oladigan so'zni tanlang.
explanation: Sinonimlar yozilishi har xil bo'lsa-da, ma'nosi yaqin bo'lgan so'zlardir. 'Small' va 'Tiny' ikkisi ham 'kichik' degan ma'noni bildiradi.

question_text: Qaysi juftlik antonimlarga (qarama-qarshi ma'noli so'zlarga) misol bo'ladi?
explanation: Antonimlar tilda zidlikni ko'rsatish uchun ishlatiladi. 'Fast' (tez) va 'Slow' (sekin) so'zlari bir-birining aksi hisoblanadi.

question_text: 'Failure' (muvaffaqiyatsizlik) so'zining antonimini toping.
explanation: Antonimlar qarama-qarshi ma'noli so'zlar bo'lib, 'Success' (muvaffaqiyat) 'Failure' (muvaffaqiyatsizlik) so'zining teskarisidir.

question_text: Agar 'Large' so'zi 'katta' degan ma'noni anglatsa, unga antonim (zid) so'z qaysi bo'lishi mumkin?
explanation: Antonimlar so'zning teskarisini ifodalaydi. 'Large' (katta) so'zining qarama-qarshisi 'Small' (kichik) so'zidir.

question_text: Qaysi holatda sinonimlardan foydalanish maqsadga muvofiq?
options: Fikrimizni chiroyli va boyroq ifodalash uchun
correct_answer: Fikrimizni chiroyli va boyroq ifodalash uchun
explanation: Kartochkaga ko'ra, sinonimlar bir narsani takrorlayvermasdan, fikrimizni chiroyli va boyroq ifodalashga yordam beradi.

question_text: Quyidagi juftliklardan qaysi biri sinonim emas?
explanation: Hot (issiq) va Cold (sovuq) so'zlari sinonim emas, ular antonimlar, ya'ni bir-biriga qarama-qarshi so'zlardir.

question_text: Nima uchun kontekstga qarab sinonim tanlash muhim?
options: Chunki barcha sinonimlar har qanday vaziyatda bir-birining o'rnini bosavermaydi
Chunki sinonimlar doimo antonim vazifasini bajaradi
Chunki sinonimlar faqat katta narsalarga nisbatan ishlatiladi
Chunki sinonimlar faqat yozma nutqda ishlatiladi
correct_answer: Chunki barcha sinonimlar har qanday vaziyatda bir-birining o'rnini bosavermaydi
explanation: Kontekstning ahamiyati shundaki, so'zlar har doim ham har qanday vaziyatda bir-birining o'rnini bosavermaydi, shuning uchun gap ma'nosiga qarab tanlash kerak.

question_text: Kontekstga ko'ra, 'Large family' iborasiga sinonim qidirilganda nimalarni hisobga olish kerak?
options: So'zlar har doim ham har qanday vaziyatda bir-birining o'rnini bosavermasligini
correct_answer: So'zlar har doim ham har qanday vaziyatda bir-birining o'rnini bosavermasligini
explanation: Kartochkada ta'kidlanganidek, 'Big' va 'Large' sinonim bo'lsa-da, 'Large family' (katta oila) tabiiyroq eshitiladi, bu esa kontekst muhimligini ko'rsatadi.

question_text: My _______ is studying at the university, and he wants to become a doctor.
explanation: Son (o'g'il) so'zi insonlarga nisbatan ishlatiladi, Sun (quyosh) esa osmon jismi. Gap mazmuni o'g'il farzand haqida ketmoqda.

question_text: Choose the correct option: I will ________ your invitation to the party.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga ko'ra, 'accept' nimanidir qabul qilish yoki rozilik bildirishni anglatadi, shuning uchun taklifni qabul qilishda aynan shu so'z ishlatiladi.

question_text: Look at those birds over _______! They are flying very high.
explanation: There so'zi joyni bildiradi. Gapda qushlarning qayerdaligini ko'rsatish uchun 'there' ishlatiladi.

question_text: The cold weather had a negative ________ on the crops.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga muvofiq, 'effect' (E - End result) natija/oqibat ma'nosida ot sifatida ishlatiladi.

question_text: The loud music did not _______ my ability to concentrate on the exam.
explanation: Affect - bu fe'l (ta'sir qilmoq). A harfi Action (harakat) bilan boshlanadi, shuning uchun bu o'rinda fe'l shakli kerak.

question_text: We walked ________ than we planned, so my legs hurt.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'farther' jismoniy, ya'ni o'lchash mumkin bo'lgan masofalar uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: She gave me a nice _______ on my new hairstyle.
explanation: Kimningdir tashqi ko'rinishini maqtash 'compliment' deb ataladi. Bu yerda maqtov haqida so'z bormoqda.

question_text: I ate everything on my plate ________ the green peas.
explanation: Kartochka 4 ga binoan, 'except' istisno qilish yoki 'tashqari' ma'nosini anglatadi.

question_text: _______ leaving for their vacation tomorrow morning.
explanation: They're - bu 'they are' iborasining qisqartmasi. Gapda 'ular ketishyapti' ma'nosi ifodalangan.

question_text: The loud noise did not ________ my concentration while I was reading.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga ko'ra, 'affect' fe'l bo'lib, ta'sir qilmoq ma'nosini anglatadi (A - Action).

question_text: The new curtains really _______ the color of the walls in the living room.
explanation: Biror narsa boshqasini to'ldirib tursa yoki mos tushsa, 'complement' ishlatiladi.

question_text: Can you explain this topic ________? I still don't fully understand it.
explanation: Kartochka 3 ga ko'ra, 'further' mavhum yoki chuqurroq tushunishni talab qiladigan masalalar uchun ishlatiladi.

question_text: The _______ of the new law was immediate; everyone started following the rules.
explanation: Effect - bu ot (natija) hisoblanadi. Gapda qonunning natijasi haqida gap ketmoqda.

question_text: Which sentence uses the homophone correctly?
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, 'two' soni (2) miqdorni bildiradi, qolgan variantlar noto'g'ri kontekstda ishlatilgan.

question_text: I can see _______ car parked in front of the library.
explanation: Their egalikni bildiradi. Bu yerda 'ularning mashinasi' haqida so'z bormoqda.

question_text: The government's new policy will ________ everyone in the country.
explanation: Kartochka 2 ga muvofiq, 'affect' fe'l bo'lib, harakatni (ta'sir qilishni) ifodalaydi.

question_text: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The weather will affect the effect of the medicine.
The weather will effect the affect of the medicine.
The weather will affect the affect of the medicine.
The weather will effect the effect of the medicine.
correct_answer: The weather will affect the effect of the medicine.
explanation: Affect (fe'l) ta'sir qilmoq, effect (ot) esa natija (ta'sir) deganidir. Havo dori natijasiga ta'sir qiladi.

question_text: Identify the sentence that correctly uses both homophones: 'to', 'too', or 'two'.
explanation: Kartochka 1 ga ko'ra, 'to' yo'nalish/fe'l oldidan keladi, 'too' esa 'ham' ma'nosini bildiradi.

question_text: Which of these is a homophone pair according to the lesson?
explanation: Homofonlar - bu aytilishi bir xil, lekin yozilishi va ma'nosi har xil so'zlar. 'Sun' va 'Son' aynan shunday talaffuz qilinadi.
